libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No Docker image, environment variable, or system property appears necessary. Do not base the fix on org.apache.pekko.http.ccompat.pre213/since213; those classes are absent from pekko-parsing_2.13:2.0.0-M1. Focus on org.apache.pekko.macros.LogHelper and LogHelperMacro. If Scala source extends or directly uses LogHelper, place the test helper in an org.apache.pekko.* package because the trait is private[pekko]; otherwise use reflection for class-loading coverage.

deterministic_setup.reason: The artifact POM declares pekko-actor_2.13 as a provided dependency, and LogHelper references org.apache.pekko.event.LoggingAdapter and Pekko annotations.
The artifact POM declares scala-reflect 2.13.18 as a provided dependency, and LogHelperMacro uses scala.reflect.macros.blackbox.Context.
Meaningful coverage of the macro API requires compiling Scala test code that invokes the def macros.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8878 [Automation] java run fails for org.apache.pekko:pekko-parsing_2.13:2.0.0-M1; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: The 2.0.0-M1 jar only contains internal macro support classes, so public API surface is very small.
Tests that exercise Scala macro expansion can be brittle across Scala/Pekko versions; keep coverage focused on current 2.0.0-M1 classes rather than removed cross-version compatibility annotations.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Pekko Parsing 2.0.0-M1 is a small internal Scala macro artifact; its usable classes reference provided Pekko Actor and Scala reflection APIs, so meaningful tests need those provided dependencies available on the test classpath.
